module1.clean_text:
RE-HOSPITALIZATION REPORT
This is the child [PATIENT] aged [AGE] years old from Mascara and residing in ORAN, from a non-consanguineous middle-aged couple, followed at our level since [DATE] for syndromic immunodeficiency type hyper IgE syndrome admitted today for his 09th venoglobulin treatment
History of illness:
Main diagnosis: hyper IgE type immunodeficiency. Associated diagnosis: epilepsy with neurodevelopmental disorder under treatment. Age of diagnosis: [AGE] years and 7 [AGE] of diagnosis: [DATE] The diagnosis of primary immunodeficiency type hyper IgE is suspected based on the following criteria:
Anamnestics:
A young death in the family. Family history of primary immunodeficiency. CLINIC:
Chronic fungal skin infections from the age of [AGE] such as onychomycosis. A personal and family atopy. Paraclinic:
Assessment of the immunodeficiency carried out on [DATE] (attached) initial finding in favor of a suspicion of an immunodeficiency type reduction of Th17 with gain of function of the STAT 1 gene. (To be completed by a phosphorylation test of the STAT 1 gene)
IgG: 6.99 g/l IgA: 0.34 g/l IgM: 0.57 g/l
IgE: 390 IU/ml (high)
TH 17: 0.23% (decreased). The STAT 1 phosphorylation test: done on: [DATE] (attached) returning: correct phosphorylation of the STAT 1 gene. The STAT 3 phosphorylation test done on: [DATE] (attached): returning in favor of correct STAT 3 phosphorylation (does not eliminate the diagnosis of hyper IgE syndrome). Hospitalization history:
Processing in progress:
Bactrim: 10 mg/kg/day. Itraconazole: 10 mg/kg/day. Events since last visit: [DATE] No
On the respiratory level: Absence of respiratory symptoms. On the digestive level: preserved transit, preserved appetite. On the skin: after-effects of whitlow lesion
Neurologically: (language and behavior): improvement in the child's language (he says words and sentences coherently, hyperactivity still present. Reason for hospitalization: admitted today for 10th course of venoglobulins. CLINICAL EXAMINATION UPON ADMISSION:
Anthropometric measurements:
Weight: 16kg500 (-0.29 DS)
Size: 99 cm (-1.59 DS)
PC: 49 cm (-0.97 SD)
BMI: (+1.14 SD)
Gomez: 97% (normal nutritional status). General condition preserved, afebrile: T° 37 C, integuments and conjunctiva normal colored. Head and neck examination: broad nasal base
ENT examination: throat: clean, no rhinorrhea or otorrhea. Examination of the oral cavity: absence of thrush or dental abscess. Pleuropulmonary examination:
Absence of thoracic deformation. Eupneic FR: 22 cycles / min. clear auscultation in both pulmonary fields. SpO2: 99% under ambient air. Cardiovascular examination: B1 B2 clearly audible, no breathing or added noises. HR: 110 bpm. Abdominopelvic examination: absence of scar or collateral circulation. Soft abdomen, absence of organomegaly, preserved transit. Genital examination: male morphotype, no anomalies of sexual differentiation. Neurological examination: conscious, cooperative child, absence of convulsions, improvement in language (he says words with sentences in a coherent manner), hyperactive child. Osteoarticular examination: ligamentous hyperlaxity, no skeletal abnormalities
Examination of the skin and appendages: sequelae of onychomycotic fungal lesions, absence of atopic dermatitis. The rest of the clinical examination was without abnormalities. BIOLOGY:
Residual IgG level 02/15/2026: 10.76 IU/ml
CAT:
Hospitalization in day hospital
Conditioning
Infusion of multivalent immunoglobulins No. 10: 0.6g/kg or 10g
Hygiene-dietary measures (therapeutic education, notebook keeping)
Close monitoring
Venoglobulin treatment completed without incident. Discharged today after end of treatment with prophylactic treatment (Bactrim and Itraconazole), and appointment for the 11th treatment. The exams requested:
follow-up report in Pediatric Neurology not yet retrieved.

phenopacket.input:
RE-HOSPITALIZATION REPORT
This is the child Bouteldja Mohamed El Amir aged 04 years old from Mascara and residing in ORAN, from a non-consanguineous middle-aged couple, followed at our level since 06/30/2025 for syndromic immunodeficiency type hyper IgE syndrome admitted today for his 09th venoglobulin treatment
History of illness:
Main diagnosis: hyper IgE type immunodeficiency. Associated diagnosis: epilepsy with neurodevelopmental disorder under treatment. Age of diagnosis: 03 years and 7 months
Date of diagnosis: 02/06/2025. The diagnosis of primary immunodeficiency type hyper IgE is suspected based on the following criteria:
Anamnestics:
A young death in the family. Family history of primary immunodeficiency. CLINIC:
Chronic fungal skin infections from the age of 9 months such as onychomycosis. A personal and family atopy. Paraclinic:
Assessment of the immunodeficiency carried out on 02/06/2025 (attached) initial finding in favor of a suspicion of an immunodeficiency type reduction of Th17 with gain of function of the STAT 1 gene. (To be completed by a phosphorylation test of the STAT 1 gene)
IgG: 6.99 g/l IgA: 0.34 g/l IgM: 0.57 g/l
IgE: 390 IU/ml (high)
TH 17: 0.23% (decreased). The STAT 1 phosphorylation test: done on: 07/21/2025 (attached) returning: correct phosphorylation of the STAT 1 gene. The STAT 3 phosphorylation test done on: 07/27/2025 (attached): returning in favor of correct STAT 3 phosphorylation (does not eliminate the diagnosis of hyper IgE syndrome). Hospitalization history:
Processing in progress:
Bactrim: 10 mg/kg/day. Itraconazole: 10 mg/kg/day. Events since last visit: 02/15/2026
Consultations: No
On the respiratory level: Absence of respiratory symptoms. On the digestive level: preserved transit, preserved appetite. On the skin: after-effects of whitlow lesion
Neurologically: (language and behavior): improvement in the child's language (he says words and sentences coherently, hyperactivity still present. Reason for hospitalization: admitted today for 10th course of venoglobulins. CLINICAL EXAMINATION UPON ADMISSION:
Anthropometric measurements:
Weight: 16kg500 (-0.29 DS)
Size: 99 cm (-1.59 DS)
PC: 49 cm (-0.97 SD)
BMI: (+1.14 SD)
Gomez: 97% (normal nutritional status). General condition preserved, afebrile: T° 37 C, integuments and conjunctiva normal colored. Head and neck examination: broad nasal base
ENT examination: throat: clean, no rhinorrhea or otorrhea. Examination of the oral cavity: absence of thrush or dental abscess. Pleuropulmonary examination:
Absence of thoracic deformation. Eupneic FR: 22 cycles / min. clear auscultation in both pulmonary fields. SpO2: 99% under ambient air. Cardiovascular examination: B1 B2 clearly audible, no breathing or added noises. HR: 110 bpm. Abdominopelvic examination: absence of scar or collateral circulation. Soft abdomen, absence of organomegaly, preserved transit. Genital examination: male morphotype, no anomalies of sexual differentiation. Neurological examination: conscious, cooperative child, absence of convulsions, improvement in language (he says words with sentences in a coherent manner), hyperactive child. Osteoarticular examination: ligamentous hyperlaxity, no skeletal abnormalities
Examination of the skin and appendages: sequelae of onychomycotic fungal lesions, absence of atopic dermatitis. The rest of the clinical examination was without abnormalities. BIOLOGY:
Residual IgG level 02/15/2026: 10.76 IU/ml
CAT:
Hospitalization in day hospital
Conditioning
Infusion of multivalent immunoglobulins No. 10: 0.6g/kg or 10g
Hygiene-dietary measures (therapeutic education, notebook keeping)
Close monitoring
Venoglobulin treatment completed without incident. Discharged today after end of treatment with prophylactic treatment (Bactrim and Itraconazole), and appointment for the 11th treatment. The exams requested:
follow-up report in Pediatric Neurology not yet retrieved.
